title: [cross-impl] TrickleTimer reset/start behavior differs across Rust/C/Python
description: Rust treats Stopped state specially in reset() and starts the timer (see test reset_from_stopped_starts_timer and try_reset logic using state==Stopped). C and Python only check interval != imin (no-op after init since interval=imin). C rpl_root.c inits trickle but never calls start(). Leads to different behavior for same RFC 6206 algorithm. Python uses _generation for async resets. See lichen/subsys/lichen/rpl/trickle.c:125, rust/lichen-rpl/src/trickle.rs:181, python/src/lichen/rpl/trickle.py:95, lichen/apps/gateway/src/rpl_root.c:27. Reviewed commits b722c4233,8ce765f9d,a8face218,f29f0c9c2 and related trickle ports.
close_reason: Cross-impl consistency fixed: reset now starts timer from stopped state in C/Python/gateway to match Rust per RFC 6206. Tests pass. Commit a850840
